QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_17

question.text: Which of the following statements about NPS Tier II is INCORRECT?

answer.text: It requires an active Tier I account
The minimum opening contribution is Rs.1,000
It allows up to 100% equity allocation
It offers tax benefits under Section 80CCD(1B)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Tier II does NOT offer tax benefits under 80CCD(1B). That applies only to Tier I. The only exception is NPS-TTS (Tier II Tax Saver) for Central Govt employees under 80C. **The Trap:** Many candidates assume Tier II has similar tax benefits as Tier I. The key difference is Tier II is a voluntary savings-cum-investment account without NPS tax incentives. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tier II features: active Tier I required, Rs.1,000 opening, Rs.250 subsequent, up to 100% equity, free withdrawals, separate nominations, no tax benefits. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Position Tier II as a liquid investment option for clients who want professional fund management at low cost (0.03-0.09% p.a.) without lock-in.
